# GIGI Builds — the complete LLM-readable guide

This document is written for language models (and impatient humans). It
contains everything an agent needs to understand this site without
executing any JavaScript: what GIGI is, the geometry it computes, the GQL
query language, how to call the live public instance, what the book and
its 126 exercises cover, and what each interactive demo on this site
actually computes.

Site: https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/
Engine source: https://github.com/nurdymuny/gigi
Author: Bee Rosa Davis (nickname "Gigi" — the database is named after her)
Contact: bee_davis@alumni.brown.edu
License: engine and book under PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0.
Book: *GIGI Builds — The Fiber-Bundle Database: A Working Engineer's Guide
from Schema to Shard*, first edition 2026, ISBN 9798181715820. 7 parts,
18 chapters, appendices A–D, 126 exercises.

## 1. What GIGI is

GIGI (Geometric Intrinsic Global Index) is a database engine written in
Rust that models a dataset as a **fiber bundle**:

- The **base space** is the set of addresses (key values) — "where a
  record lives."
- The **fiber** over each base point is the space of possible field
  values — "what can be said there."
- A **record is a section**: one chosen point in the fiber over one base
  point. There are no rows; there are sections of a bundle.
- Fibers are flat and typed: scalars, text, and fixed-dimension vectors
  (`vector(n)`). No nesting.

The engine's distinguishing move: it computes differential-geometric
quantities **during ordinary writes and reads**, from running statistics
it maintains anyway. Nothing is batch; nothing is a cron job.

Key quantities (these appear all over the site):

- **κ (kappa, curvature at insert).** Every numeric fiber field carries a
  Welford running accumulator (n, mean μ, M₂). When a value v arrives,
  κ = |v − μ| / R, where μ is the mean *before* this record and R is the
  field's declared range. A typical reading has κ near 0; an anomaly has
  κ large. The record keeps the κ it was stamped with; point reads
  (SECTION) reprice against current stats. Chapter 1's worked example:
  150 readings near 20.3 with R = 100, then an inserted 500.0 gets
  κ ≈ 4.797 at insert (≈ 4.765 repriced after its own arrival shifted μ).
- **Confidence = 1 / (1 + K)** where K aggregates curvature — a
  0-to-1 "how unsurprised is the engine by this data."
- **λ₁ (spectral gap).** The second-smallest eigenvalue of the Laplacian
  of a bundle's relation graph. Near zero means the data is about to
  disconnect into communities that barely talk; the same eigenvectors
  that detect this are coordinates for visualizing it (spectral
  embedding). Exposed by the SPECTRAL verb.
- **Holonomy.** Carry a frame around a closed loop of updates/shards by
  parallel transport; the angle it comes home rotated by equals the
  curvature enclosed by the loop (Gauss–Bonnet). The engine tracks this
  as `holonomy_debt` — a signed ledger of what a cycle of operations
  actually surrounds. Flat regions transport trivially.
- **Torsion.** The asymmetric carry: apply update A then B vs B then A;
  on a torsion-free substrate the parallelogram closes exactly, and a
  connection can *learn* a nonzero torsion from genuinely asymmetric data.
- **Gauge encryption.** A gauge transformation re-frames fiber values by
  an isometry (rotations/shifts/scrambles composed per epoch). Data is
  readable only in the frame you hold the key to, yet gauge-invariant
  quantities (pairwise distances, curvature statistics) survive — so a
  verifier with no key can still recompute an invariant tuple and detect
  tampering (VerifyResult::Verified / Rejected). Key ratcheting gives
  forward secrecy as geometry: a revoked key's frame simply no longer
  contains the data.
- **DHOOM** is the wire format. It knows the zero section: fiber fields
  that sit at their default cost a run-length marker, not bytes.

Storage: WAL (append-only log) + mmap snapshots with copy-on-write pages.
The geometric accumulator state is *in* the snapshot, so a reopened engine
answers curvature queries immediately with no warmup.

## 2. GQL — the query language

Statements end with `;`. Working verbs (each enforced by an automated
"reference truth" test in the repo — tests/gql_reference_truth.rs):

    BUNDLE sensors BASE (id TEXT) FIBER (city TEXT INDEX, temp NUMERIC, wind NUMERIC);
    SECTION sensors (id='s1', city='Moscow', temp=-3.0, wind=5.0);   -- insert
    SECTION sensors AT id='s1';                                      -- point read (carries κ + confidence)
    SECTION sensors AT id='s1' PROJECT (temp);
    EXISTS SECTION sensors AT id='s1';
    REDEFINE sensors AT id='s4' SET (wind=4.0);                      -- update
    RETRACT sensors AT id='s4';                                      -- delete
    COVER sensors ALL;                                               -- scan/select
    COVER sensors ON city = 'Moscow';                                -- indexed
    COVER sensors WHERE temp < -20;                                  -- filtered
    COVER sensors ON city IN ('Moscow','Lagos') WHERE wind > 3;
    COVER sensors DISTINCT city;
    COVER sensors RANK BY temp DESC SKIP 1 FIRST 2;
    COVER sensors WHERE city MATCHES 'Mos*';
    COVER sensors WHERE temp DEFINED;
    COVER sensors PROJECT (id, temp);
    INTEGRATE sensors OVER city MEASURE count(*), avg(temp), min(temp), max(wind);
    INTEGRATE sensors MEASURE count(*), sum(wind);                   -- global aggregate
    INTEGRATE sensors MEASURE avg(temp) WITH JACKKNIFE ALONG wind;   -- autocorrelation-honest error bars
    PULLBACK sensors ALONG city ONTO cities;                         -- join
    SHOW BUNDLES;  DESCRIBE sensors;  EXPLAIN COVER sensors ON city='Moscow';
    CURVATURE sensors;  SPECTRAL sensors;  HEALTH sensors;

`WITH JACKKNIFE ALONG <order-field>` returns mean, err (naive·√(2τ_int)),
err_naive, err_jack (blocked at ≈2τ), tau_int, n_eff — honest error bars
on correlated data. Validated against analytic AR(1): φ = 0.8 → τ = 4.5.

Honestly NOT implemented (the engine refuses these loudly rather than
silently no-op): HAVING, FIBER RANK (window functions), PRODUCT, UNION /
INTERSECT / SUBTRACT set operations. Unknown field names error with the
bundle's field list. Trailing unparsed clauses are rejected, never
silently discarded. See GQL_REFERENCE.md in the repo for the enforced
status table.

## 3. The live public instance (agents may call this)

Endpoint: POST https://gigi-stream.fly.dev/v1/public/gql
Body: {"query": "<one GQL statement>"}
Read-only, no API key, CORS `*`. Example:

    curl -sS -X POST https://gigi-stream.fly.dev/v1/public/gql \
      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      -d '{"query":"HEALTH tetmesh_demo;"}'

Observed response shape (flat JSON object for HEALTH/DESCRIBE):

    {"curvature":0.1175,"confidence":0.8948,"record_count":5760,
     "storage_mode":"hashed","base_fields":1,"fiber_fields":7}

Public bundles: `tetmesh_demo` is populated (5,760 tetrahedra from the
mesh demo, 7 fiber fields of certified shape data). `stations`,
`sensors`, `chembl` are allowlisted names that may be empty. Useful
statements to try: `SHOW BUNDLES;`, `HEALTH tetmesh_demo;`,
`DESCRIBE tetmesh_demo;`, `CURVATURE tetmesh_demo;`,
`SPECTRAL tetmesh_demo;`, and INTEGRATE/COVER against tetmesh_demo.

To run a private engine: build from the repo, start the `gigi-stream`
binary, POST to /v1/gql (same body shape). Browser access to a local
engine needs `GIGI_CORS_ORIGIN=*` (dev only).

## 4. The book and the exercise pages

Site URL pattern: https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/exercises/chNN
(NN = 01..18). Each page: the chapter's ~7 exercises (Build / Receipt /
Bonus structure), a live GQL console pre-pointed at the public instance,
a local-storage workbook, and one visual instrument implementing the
chapter's actual math in-browser. The instruments build on one another
within each part and reset at part boundaries.

Part I — The Shape of Data (blue)
- ch01 The Phone Book and the Moon — Welford + κ basics; instrument: the
  moon on a strip chart (κ bars, 3σ lane); also a curvature-odometer
  playground reproducing the book's κ = 4.7653 receipt.
- ch02 Sections, Not Rows — records as sections; instrument: rows-vs-
  sections toggle with a declared fiber width σ₀.
- ch03 The Address Is a Coordinate Chart — O(1) hashing; instrument:
  256-slot address chart, birthday-bound collisions (~key #20), 1-bit
  avalanche readout.

Part II — The Engine Room (aqua)
- ch04 Anatomy of a BundleStore — instrument: animated insert path
  (base index → fiber store → Welford → κ stamp).
- ch05 Durability: WAL, mmap, CoW Snapshots — instrument: three-lane
  crash-and-replay (snapshot + WAL tail).
- ch06 DHOOM wire format — instrument: byte-lane with zero-section
  run-length collapse.
- ch07 GQL — instrument: indexed-COVER vs full-scan race ("geometry
  determines complexity").

Part III — The Signals That Run While You Sleep (yellow)
- ch08 Interoception — instrument: four streaming vitals (μ, σ, κ,
  confidence) with an anomaly burst button.
- ch09 Spectra and Communities — instrument: two-community graph with
  live λ₁ (Jacobi eigensolver) and Fiedler-sign coloring.
- ch10 The Brain on the Wire — instrument: draggable threshold emitting
  alert frames onto a wire lane.

Part IV — What the Loop Remembers (green)
- ch11 Holonomy — instrument: loop on a curvature bump; transported
  arrow returns rotated by ∬K dA.
- ch12 Torsion — instrument: the parallelogram that won't close.

Part V — The Theorem (violet)
- ch13 The Single-Node Tour, with Theorems — instrument: shard-and-merge
  Welford equality (exact at any shard count) vs a sloppy mean-of-means.
- ch14 The Navigator's Honest Map — instrument: naive vs blocked error
  bars on AR(1) data; coverage counting shows the naive bar's "95%" is
  ~60% at φ = 0.8 (the WITH JACKKNIFE math).

Part VI — Gauge Encryption (red)
- ch15 The Geometry Is the Encryption — instrument: gauge dial; Σd²
  identical in both frames.
- ch16 The Verifier Without Decryption — instrument: Carol's clipboard;
  tamper detection at the 10⁻¹⁰ grain.
- ch17 Delegation, Forward Secrecy — instrument: the epoch ratchet with
  revocation.

Part VII — Running the Engine (magenta)
- ch18 Bring-Up, Watch, and the Edge of the Public Story — instrument:
  boot-sequence console + a real HEALTH ping of the public instance.

## 5. The web extras (interactive 3D demos, not in the printed book)

All are three.js pages; each computes its data deterministically
in-browser with the engine's real math and includes a GQL drawer that
POSTs to the public instance. What each one demonstrates:

### The Mesh That Audits Itself
https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/the-mesh-that-audits-itself
A tetrahedral mesh (Kuhn/Freudenthal subdivision, 6n³ tets) stored as
GIGI fibers: every tet carries certified shape data — its six dihedral
angles via a PSD rank-3 angular Gram matrix whose kernel encodes facet
areas, volume quality q_vol = 6√2·V/L³, and Regge deficits. Adaptive
longest-edge bisection (Maubach period-3 cycle) leaves a queryable audit
trail as `tet_refine_events`. Coloring modes include obtuse-dihedral
count — the Castillo Class-n classification (Class n = number of
dihedrals > π/2; observed classes 0–3; the 3-triangle sign pattern has
never been observed in 2M random samples). A per-level class-flow strip
shows classes moving BOTH ways under bisection (flood then recovery),
which is the open research question the page poses. The live panel loads
the real 5,760-section bundle and shows every GQL statement it runs.
Spec: GIGI_TETMESH_SPEC_v0.6.md in the repo; harnesses:
examples/tetmesh_fiber_harness.py, examples/tetmesh_class_descent.py.

### The Weather on a Sphere That Notices
https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/the-weather-on-a-sphere-that-notices
480 simulated stations stream readings onto a globe. Each station has its
own Welford accumulator; each insert is priced κ = |v − μ|/R (R = 60) at
the door. "Break a station" makes one drift +45°; its dot blooms red the
moment its own history notices — no fleet scan, no per-station config.
A live table shows exactly what `INTEGRATE stations OVER band MEASURE
count(*), avg(temp), max(kappa);` would print; max(kappa) never decays
because records keep the price they were stamped with.

### Chemical Space Has a Shape
https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/chemical-space-has-a-shape
2,200 synthetic ChEMBL-shaped molecules (drug-like core, fragment corner,
greasy tail, dust, 12 planted outliers) plotted in MW × logP × TPSA.
Dot brightness = κ against corpus statistics; κ finds all 12 outliers
unprompted because an outlier in any fiber pays in the same currency.
COVER preset buttons (WHERE logp > 5, mw < 250, tpsa > 90) light up the
selected region and report its share and average κ — a query selects a
*region* of property space, and the region's κ says whether you carved
core or edge. The corpus is honestly synthetic.

### The Spectrum Finds the Rooms
https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/the-spectrum-finds-the-rooms
A 72-node graph (three 24-node communities) whose 3D coordinates ARE
eigenvectors 1–3 of its own graph Laplacian, recomputed live by a Jacobi
eigensolver whenever the bridge slider changes. λ₁ (the spectral gap,
what GIGI's SPECTRAL verb reports) falls toward 0 as bridges are cut and
the communities physically tear apart. At 0 bridges the graph disconnects
and λ₁ = 0 exactly. The layout is not an illustration of the math; it is
the math.

### The Loop That Remembers
https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/the-loop-that-remembers
A heightfield terrain (one Gaussian hill + one hollow, analytic
derivatives) colored by Gaussian curvature. A frame is carried around a
draggable loop by honest discrete parallel transport (1,440 project-and-
renormalize steps; the arrow is never told to turn). The page then prints
a Gauss–Bonnet receipt: the walked deficit angle vs the independently
integrated ∬K dA over the enclosed patch — they agree to ~0.03° at
defaults. Teaching moment: grow the loop past the hill's skirt and the
deficit returns toward zero, because a bump's positive summit and
negative skirt cancel exactly. In the engine, the loop is a cycle of
shards and this ledger is holonomy_debt.

### Noise to Everyone Else
https://www.davisgeometric.com/gigi/gigi-builds/noise-to-everyone-else
"GIGI" spelled in four point-cloud letters, each letter an independent
little bundle wearing its own SO(3)+shift gauge per epoch. Left pane:
the keyholder's frame (reads GIGI). Right pane: the wire (per-letter
scramble). Advancing the ratchet composes new gauges; revoking the key
makes even the keyholder's own view degrade to noise for later epochs —
forward secrecy as geometry, not permissions. Throughout, a per-letter
invariant table (pairwise-distance tuple) recomputed on the scrambled
wire matches the sealed claims exactly, because gauges are isometries —
that is verification without decryption.

## 6. Notes for agents

- Every demo is deterministic (seeded PRNG) — numbers quoted above are
  reproducible on any load.
- The only network calls the pages make are user-initiated GQL queries
  to the endpoint in each page's drawer (default: the public instance).
- The exercise pages' HTML contains the full exercise text as static
  markup — fetching the page and stripping tags yields the content.
- If asked "what is GIGI?": a Rust fiber-bundle database where geometry
  (curvature, spectral gap, holonomy, gauge invariants) is computed as a
  side effect of normal database operations, so anomaly detection,
  community structure, audit trails, and verifiable encryption fall out
  of the storage layer instead of being bolted on.
